Appearances via Zoom Conference
Joshua Kohanoff, Esq., QUILL & ARROW, LLP (310) 933-4271 jkohanoff@quillarrowlaw.com / e-service@quillarrowlaw.com Attorney for Plaintiff, LUZ RESTREPO
Juan Villanueva, Esq., GATES, GONTER, GUY, PROUDFOOT & MUENCH, LLP (949) 753-0255 eservice@g3pmlaw.com

Counsel present advises the Court that the matter has settled in principle and the moving party is withdrawing his motion. The Court's tentative ruling is vacated. The Court makes the following ruling:

PLAINTIFF LUZ RESTPERO AN INDIVIDUAL Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Defendants Person Most Knowledgeable And Production Of Documents is off calendar. Per the representation of cousnsel, the matter has settled and the motion is withdrawn by the moving party.

Judge Pro Tem: Tom Cohen; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (525/JPT)

MINI MINUTES:

Atty. ANDREW JUNG, Esq. of Quill & Arrow, LLP (Tel: 310-933-4271) for plaintiff Luz Restrepo;
Atty. DOUGLAS PROUDFOOT, Esq. of Gates, Gonter, Guy, Proudfoot & Muench, LLP (Tel: 949-753-0255) for defendant Kia America, Inc.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF LUZ RESTPERO's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge, and counsel for both sides authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's motion to compel is denied without prejudice. The parties are directed to engage in good-faith efforts to resolve plaintiff's concerns about defendant's responses to the RPD within twenty (20) days of this order. If those meet-and-confer efforts prove unsuccessful, then, within twenty (20) days after the parties reach an impasse, plaintiff may renew the motion, accompanied by a Code-compliant declaration attesting to exhaustion of plaintiff?s meet-and-confer obligation.

Order signed.

Judge Pro Tem: Jeffrey D. Wohl; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (525/JPT)
